plan: Step 1: Install dependencies — run `npm install` to set up node_modules (including @playwright/test and playwright) and then `npx playwright install chromium` to download the Chromium browser binary needed for e2e tests.

Step 2: Verify existing e2e tests pass — run `npm run test:e2e` to confirm the existing 5 spec files (default-page.spec.ts, app-shell.spec.ts, renderer.spec.ts, presets.spec.ts, probe-sampler.spec.ts) all pass. These already cover all 4 acceptance criteria: app loads (default-page + app-shell), preset switching (presets.spec.ts), canvas visible (app-shell + renderer), and tests run from npm script (test:e2e script exists).

Step 3: Create a unified smoke test file — add `tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts` that validates the integrated app experience end-to-end in a single test suite: (a) app loads without errors, (b) canvas #cathedral is visible and has rendered pixel data, (c) all 3 preset buttons are visible and clickable, (d) switching presets moves the active class, (e) canvas continues rendering after preset switch (no crash). This consolidates the smoke-test narrative for US-017 specifically.

Step 4: Add a canvas-after-preset-switch assertion — in the new smoke file, after clicking each preset button, verify the canvas still has non-zero pixel data (three.js didn't crash on preset change). This is a gap in existing tests which test presets and rendering separately but not the interaction.
Step 5: Run full quality gate — execute `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test && npm run test:e2e` to confirm all checks pass including the new smoke test.
Step 6: Update progress log and mark ticket passes=true — update the US-017 ticket status to reflect that all acceptance criteria are met.
testing_recommendations: Run `npm run test:e2e` and verify all existing e2e specs pass (default-page, app-shell, renderer, presets, probe-sampler) plus the new smoke.spec.ts
Verify the new smoke test covers the integrated flow: load → canvas visible → preset click → canvas still rendering
Run the full quality gate: `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test && npm run test:e2e`
Confirm `npx playwright test --list` shows all expected test cases including the new smoke suite
Manually run with `--headed` flag to visually confirm the app loads and presets switch in the browser
risks: node_modules are missing in the worktree — npm install must succeed before anything else works
Playwright Chromium binary may not be installed — requires `npx playwright install chromium` which downloads ~150MB
WebGL may not be available in headless Chromium on CI — existing renderer.spec.ts pixel-data test has a fallback via toDataURL but this could be flaky in some environments
The dev server (vite on port 5173) must start reliably for all e2e tests — playwright.config.ts already handles this with webServer config but port conflicts could cause failures

Existing e2e tests from prior tickets (US-003, US-004, US-006, US-007) already substantially cover the acceptance criteria — the main risk is that US-017 becomes redundant rather than additive; the new smoke.spec.ts addresses this by testing the integrated preset→render flow
